api/client/python/openmeter/_generated/a.purpose: Async (asyncio) mirror of the sync operations layer — each Operations class wraps a resource group and exposes `async def` methods that execute HTTP requests via AsyncPipelineClient, delegating all request-building to sync `build_*` functions in the parent `operations/_operations.py`.

patterns.name: Delegate request construction to sync build_* functions
patterns.description: Every async method imports and calls `build_<resource>_<action>_request` from `...operations._operations` — never duplicates URL or query-param construction inline.
patterns.example: _request = build_apps_list_request(page=page, page_size=page_size, headers=_headers, params=_params)

patterns.name: Positional constructor injection
patterns.description: Each Operations class `__init__` pops (client, config, serializer, deserializer) from positional args first, kwargs second — never as named-only parameters.
patterns.example: self._client: AsyncPipelineClient = input_args.pop(0) if input_args else kwargs.pop('client')

patterns.name: Per-method error_map with typed model deserialization
patterns.description: Every method builds a MutableMapping error_map seeded with 404/409/304 defaults, then manually deserializes typed Problem models (BadRequestProblemResponse, UnauthorizedProblemResponse, etc.) for each status code before raising HttpResponseError.
patterns.example: error = _failsafe_deserialize(_models.BadRequestProblemResponse, response); raise HttpResponseError(response=response, model=error)

patterns.name: Stream-safe response body cleanup on error
patterns.description: On non-2xx responses when `_stream=True`, call `await response.read()` inside a try/except for StreamConsumedError and StreamClosedError before calling map_error — prevents socket leaks.
patterns.example: if _stream: try: await response.read() except (StreamConsumedError, StreamClosedError): pass

patterns.name: _patch.py customization hook
patterns.description: `_patch.py` is the only sanctioned place to add customizations; `__init__.py` imports `from ._patch import *` and calls `_patch_sdk()` at module load. Keep `_patch.py` minimal — it is the escape hatch, not the norm.
patterns.example: from ._patch import __all__ as _patch_all; _patch_sdk()

key_file_guides.role: Contains every async Operations class and their async methods. Generated file — do not edit directly; changes belong in _patch.py or upstream generators.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do NOT instantiate Operations classes directly; they are accessed through OpenMeterClient attributes. Adding new methods here without a corresponding sync build_* function in the parent operations package breaks the reuse pattern.

key_file_guides.role: Re-exports all Operations classes and applies _patch.py overrides via `_patch_sdk()`. Mirrors the sync operations __init__.py exactly.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Any new Operations class added to _operations.py must be added to both __all__ and the import list here, and mirrored in the sync __init__.py.

key_file_guides.role: Empty stub for SDK customizations; imported and executed by __init__.py. Preserved across regenerations as the intentional customization seam.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never add business logic here. This file's sole purpose is to be the stable customization point that survives code regeneration.

anti_patterns: Duplicating URL/query-param construction inside async methods instead of calling the shared sync build_* functions
Instantiating Operations classes directly rather than accessing them via OpenMeterClient attributes
Adding error handling or deserialization logic outside the per-method error_map block
Editing _operations.py as if it were hand-written code — it is generated and will be overwritten on next SDK generation
Raising exceptions before calling `await response.read()` on a streaming error response (leaks the socket)

decisions.decision: Async operations delegate all request construction to sync build_* functions in the parent package
decisions.rationale: Avoids duplicating URL and parameter logic across sync/async variants; a single change to the request builder propagates to both execution paths.

decisions.decision: Per-method typed error deserialization instead of a shared error handler
decisions.rationale: Different endpoints return different Problem subtypes; inline deserialization keeps error context close to the operation contract without requiring a shared dispatcher that must know all error shapes.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new async operation method following the existing pattern

api/v3/handlers/meters.purpose: v3 HTTP handler package for all meter CRUD and query operations; bridges generated api/v3 request/response types to meter.ManageService and streaming.Connector via the httptransport.Handler pipeline, and owns the query/ sub-package for translating MeterQueryRequest fields into streaming.QueryParams.

patterns.name: Type-alias triplets per operation file
patterns.description: Each operation file declares three type aliases: <Op>Request, <Op>Response, <Op>Handler. Request/Response alias domain input/output types directly; Handler aliases httptransport.Handler or HandlerWithArgs.
patterns.example: type CreateMeterRequest = meter.CreateMeterInput; type CreateMeterResponse = api.Meter; type CreateMeterHandler httptransport.Handler[CreateMeterRequest, CreateMeterResponse]

patterns.name: NewHandlerWithArgs for path-param endpoints; NewHandler for no-param
patterns.description: Endpoints with a meterID path param use httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs. Endpoints without path params (e.g. CreateMeter) use httptransport.NewHandler.
patterns.example: httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs(decoderFunc, operationFunc, commonhttp.JSONResponseEncoderWithStatus[Response](http.StatusOK), opts...)

patterns.name: Namespace resolved in decoder, never in operation
patterns.description: h.resolveNamespace(ctx) is always called inside the decoder closure (first argument); errors from it are returned immediately without entering the operation.
patterns.example: ns, err := h.resolveNamespace(ctx); if err != nil { return Request{}, err }

patterns.name: apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder on every handler
patterns.description: Every httptransport.NewHandler / NewHandlerWithArgs call must append httptransport.WithErrorEncoder(apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder()) inside httptransport.AppendOptions.
patterns.example: httptransport.AppendOptions(h.options, httptransport.WithOperationName("get-meter"), httptransport.WithErrorEncoder(apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder()))...

patterns.name: Reserved dimension validation in decoder
patterns.description: For Create and Update, call validateDimensionsWithoutReserved(*body.Dimensions) in the decoder before constructing the domain input; this delegates to query.IsReservedDimension per key.
patterns.example: if body.Dimensions != nil { if err := validateDimensionsWithoutReserved(*body.Dimensions); err != nil { return Request{}, err } }

patterns.name: Query endpoints delegate to query.BuildQueryParams
patterns.description: Both QueryMeter and QueryMeterCSV call query.BuildQueryParams(ctx, m, req.Body, query.NewCustomerResolver(h.customerService)) — never inline param-building logic in the handler files.
patterns.example: params, err := query.BuildQueryParams(ctx, m, req.Body, query.NewCustomerResolver(h.customerService))

patterns.name: Goverter-generated conversion vars initialized in init()
patterns.description: convert.gen.go sets package-level var functions (FromAPICreateMeterRequest, ToAPIMeter, etc.) inside init(). Never call them before init runs and never hand-edit convert.gen.go.
patterns.example: var FromAPICreateMeterRequest func(namespace string, req api.CreateMeterRequest) (meter.CreateMeterInput, error)

key_file_guides.role: Defines the Handler interface (all 7 method signatures) and the handler struct with resolveNamespace, service, streaming, customerService, options. New() injects all dependencies.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not add domain service calls to handler struct methods directly; those belong in per-operation files.

key_file_guides.role: Hand-written helpers (ToAPIMeterAggregation, FromAPIMeterAggregation, ToAPIMeterQueryRow, ToAPIMeterQueryResult, FromAPIUpdateMeterRequest) plus Goverter annotations driving convert.gen.go generation.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Goverter var declarations (FromAPICreateMeterRequest, ToAPIMeter) must not be called manually before init(); they are set in convert.gen.go's init().

key_file_guides.role: Generated by Goverter — DO NOT EDIT. Contains init() that sets all package-level conversion function vars.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Any hand-edit is overwritten by 'go generate ./...'. Re-run make generate after changing convert.go annotations.

key_file_guides.role: Declares ErrCodeReservedDimension ErrorCode constant and ErrReservedDimension ValidationIssue sentinel with HTTP 400 attribute. NewReservedDimensionError adds path and value attributes.
key_file_guides.watch_for: All new validation errors in this package must follow the same pattern: package-level sentinel + constructor that calls .WithPathString/.WithAttr.

key_file_guides.role: validateDimensionsWithoutReserved iterates a map and returns NewReservedDimensionError if any key matches query.IsReservedDimension.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New reserved dimensions must be added in query/dimensions.go (IsReservedDimension), not here.

key_file_guides.role: QueryMeterCSV handler plus queryMeterCSVResult implementing commonhttp.CSVResponse. Column order: from, to, [subject,] [customer_id, customer_key, customer_name,] <other dims>, value. Customer enrichment via ListCustomers post-query.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Any new reserved dimension column must be added to both the header slice and the record-building loop in Records().

key_file_guides.role: Sub-package owning BuildQueryParams, IsReservedDimension, CustomerResolverFunc, filter operator extraction, and ISO 8601 to WindowSize lookup.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not inline query parameter translation in the parent handler files; always delegate to BuildQueryParams.

anti_patterns: Using httptransport.NewHandler instead of NewHandlerWithArgs when the endpoint takes a path parameter (meterID).
Calling h.resolveNamespace in the operation closure instead of the decoder closure.
Adding a new reserved dimension key directly in this package instead of updating query.IsReservedDimension in query/dimensions.go.
Hand-editing convert.gen.go — it is overwritten by go generate.
Omitting httptransport.WithErrorEncoder(apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder()) from the options block, which breaks domain-error-to-HTTP-status mapping.

decisions.decision: query/ is a separate sub-package rather than inline code in query.go
decisions.rationale: Isolates all MeterQueryRequest to streaming.QueryParams translation (dimension validation, filter extraction, customer resolution, window-size mapping) so handlers stay thin and the logic is independently testable without an HTTP context.

decisions.decision: Conversion functions are Goverter-generated vars initialized in init(), not methods
decisions.rationale: Goverter generates type-safe mapping code at build time; package-level vars set in init() let the generated code live in a separate file while being callable from hand-written handlers without a receiver.

Pre-building the base issue (ErrReservedDimension) as a sentinel lets the HTTP error encoder read the embedded HTTP status code attribute, while per-call constructors add field-specific path/value context without duplicating the base message.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new CRUD endpoint (e.g. CloneMeter) following the established handler pattern

api/v3/handlers/plans.purpose: v3 HTTP handler package for all plan CRUD and lifecycle operations (create, get, list, update, delete, publish, archive); bridges generated api/v3 types to plan.Service with rich bidirectional conversion for the Plan > Phase > RateCard > Price hierarchy.

patterns.name: Type-alias triplets per operation file
patterns.description: Each operation file declares <Op>Request, <Op>Response, <Op>Handler type aliases. Request/Response alias domain input/output types directly.
patterns.example: type CreatePlanRequest = plan.CreatePlanInput; type CreatePlanResponse = api.BillingPlan; type CreatePlanHandler httptransport.Handler[CreatePlanRequest, CreatePlanResponse]

patterns.name: NewHandlerWithArgs for path-param endpoints; NewHandler for no-param
patterns.description: Endpoints with a planID path param use NewHandlerWithArgs; CreatePlan (no path param) uses NewHandler.
patterns.example: httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs(decoderFunc, operationFunc, commonhttp.JSONResponseEncoderWithStatus[Response](http.StatusOK), opts...)

patterns.name: Namespace resolved first in decoder
patterns.description: h.resolveNamespace(ctx) is always the first call in the decoder closure; return immediately on error.
patterns.example: ns, err := h.resolveNamespace(ctx); if err != nil { return Request{}, err }

patterns.name: IgnoreNonCriticalIssues=true on create and update
patterns.description: After FromAPICreatePlanRequest / FromAPIUpsertPlanRequest, set req.IgnoreNonCriticalIssues = true before returning from the decoder.
patterns.example: req, err := FromAPICreatePlanRequest(ns, body); ...; req.IgnoreNonCriticalIssues = true; return req, nil

patterns.name: clock.Now() for time-sensitive fields in decoder
patterns.description: PublishPlan sets EffectiveFrom = clock.Now(); ArchivePlan sets EffectiveTo = clock.Now(). Never use time.Now() — tests mock clock via clock.SetTime.
patterns.example: EffectiveFrom: lo.ToPtr(clock.Now())

patterns.name: Nil-check on service response for mutating lifecycle operations
patterns.description: CreatePlan, UpdatePlan, PublishPlan, ArchivePlan check for p == nil after the service call and return a descriptive error. Get/Delete do not need this check.
patterns.example: if p == nil { return CreatePlanResponse{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to create plan") }

patterns.name: Exhaustive price type switch with unsupported-type guard
patterns.description: ToAPIBillingPrice uses an exhaustive switch on p.Type(). DynamicPriceType and PackagePriceType return models.NewGenericConflictError. ListPlans silently skips plans with unsupported prices via hasUnsupportedV3Price.
patterns.example: case productcatalog.DynamicPriceType: return result, models.NewGenericConflictError(fmt.Errorf("dynamic price is not supported in v3 API"))

key_file_guides.role: Defines Handler interface (7 method signatures) and handler struct with resolveNamespace, service (plan.Service), and options. New() constructor. Only plan.Service is injected — no streaming or customer service dependency.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not inject additional services here; if you need customer lookups, delegate to plan.Service or add a dedicated helper.

key_file_guides.role: All bidirectional conversion: ToAPIBillingPlan, ToAPIBillingPlanPhase, ToAPIBillingRateCard, ToAPIBillingPrice, ToAPIBillingPriceTiers, FromAPICreatePlanRequest, FromAPIUpsertPlanRequest, FromAPIBillingPlanPhase, FromAPIBillingRateCard, FromAPIBillingPrice, ToProRatingConfig.
key_file_guides.watch_for: DynamicPriceType and PackagePriceType return GenericConflictError (not 500). Adding a new price type requires updating ToAPIBillingPrice, FromAPIBillingPrice, and the unsupportedV3PriceTypes map.

key_file_guides.role: Table-driven unit tests for all convert.go functions. Uses clock.SetTime for status tests, newTestPlan helper, and decimal/gobl/currency literals.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New conversion paths must have corresponding table-driven tests here; use assert.Equal with typed API constants (api.BillingPlanStatus*, api.ISO8601Duration, api.Numeric) rather than plain strings.

key_file_guides.role: ListPlans with page-based pagination (default page 1, size 20). Silently skips plans with unsupported v3 prices via hasUnsupportedV3Price.
key_file_guides.watch_for: The hasUnsupportedV3Price skip is a temporary workaround (FIXME). Do not add further silent-skip logic — return errors or expose all items.

key_file_guides.role: PublishPlan sets EffectiveFrom = clock.Now() in the decoder.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must use clock.Now() not time.Now() — tests control clock via clock.SetTime.

key_file_guides.role: ArchivePlan sets EffectiveTo = clock.Now() in the decoder.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Same clock.Now() requirement as publish.go.

anti_patterns: Using time.Now() instead of clock.Now() in decoder closures — breaks time-controlled tests.
Omitting req.IgnoreNonCriticalIssues = true in Create/Update decoders — rejects plans with non-critical validation issues.
Adding a new price type in ToAPIBillingPrice without also updating FromAPIBillingPrice, hasUnsupportedV3Price, and the round-trip tests in convert_test.go.
Omitting httptransport.WithErrorEncoder(apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder()) from any handler options block.
Calling domain service methods from the encoder closure (third argument) — all service calls must happen in the operation closure (second argument).

decisions.decision: DynamicPriceType and PackagePriceType return GenericConflictError (409) rather than a 500
decisions.rationale: The v3 API does not yet have a wire format for these price types; a conflict error surfaces the limitation clearly to callers instead of silently dropping data or returning a generic server error.

decisions.decision: Labels round-trip via labels.ToMetadata / labels.FromMetadata, not direct map assignment
decisions.rationale: The labels package enforces key/value format rules and provides consistent nil-vs-empty handling across all handlers that touch label metadata.

decisions.decision: IgnoreNonCriticalIssues set in the HTTP decoder, not in the domain service
decisions.rationale: HTTP create/update callers expect lenient validation; the service layer retains the ability to surface non-critical issues as warnings on the returned object without blocking persistence.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new plan lifecycle action (e.g. ClonePlan) with clock.Now() and nil-check

api/v3/handlers/subscriptions.purpose: v3 HTTP handler package for billing subscription lifecycle (list, get, create from plan, cancel, unschedule cancellation, change-plan); uses two distinct domain services — planSubscriptionService for create/change workflows and subscriptionService for get/list/cancel/continue.

patterns.name: Pre-validation of referenced entities inside the decoder
patterns.description: Resolve and validate customer and plan references inside the request decoder (first func) using apierrors.NewBadRequestError with InvalidParameters for missing required fields.
patterns.example: if body.Plan.Id == nil && body.Plan.Key == nil { return ..., apierrors.NewBadRequestError(ctx, errors.New(reason), []apierrors.InvalidParameter{{Field: "plan.id", ...}}) }

patterns.name: Private helper methods for ID-or-key entity lookup
patterns.description: getCustomerByIDOrKey and getPlanByIDOrKey are private handler methods that accept namespace + optional ID/Key pointers and return the entity. They are defined in create.go and reused by change.go.
patterns.example: customerEntity, err := h.getCustomerByIDOrKey(ctx, ns, body.Customer.Id, body.Customer.Key)

patterns.name: PlanInput constructed via FromRef, never direct field assignment
patterns.description: Always construct plansubscription.PlanInput via planInput.FromRef(&PlanRefInput{Key: ..., Version: ...}) after resolving the concrete plan.
patterns.example: planInput := plansubscription.PlanInput{}; planInput.FromRef(&plansubscription.PlanRefInput{Key: planEntity.Key, Version: &planEntity.Version})

patterns.name: Timing union decode: AsDateTime() before AsBillingSubscriptionEditTimingEnum()
patterns.description: FromAPIBillingSubscriptionEditTiming tries AsDateTime() first; enum fallback is second. This order is critical because datetime strings also satisfy the string union type.
patterns.example: if custom, err := t.AsDateTime(); err == nil { return subscription.Timing{Custom: &custom}, nil }

patterns.name: planSubscriptionService for create/change; subscriptionService for get/list/cancel/continue
patterns.description: The handler struct holds two subscription services with different responsibilities. Using the wrong one silently changes workflow semantics and billing sync.
patterns.example: h.planSubscriptionService.Create(ctx, request) // not h.subscriptionService.Create

key_file_guides.role: Handler interface (6 methods) and handler struct: resolveNamespace, customerService, planService, planSubscriptionService, subscriptionService.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Two distinct subscription services are injected: planSubscriptionService (create/change) and subscriptionService (get/list/cancel/continue). Mixing them changes workflow semantics.

key_file_guides.role: Creates subscription from plan; validates customer and plan exist, builds PlanInput via FromRef, calls planSubscriptionService.Create. Also defines getCustomerByIDOrKey and getPlanByIDOrKey private helpers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: TODO comments note helpers should eventually move to the service layer — keep them private to handler for now.

key_file_guides.role: Changes running subscription to a new plan; fetches current subscription for name/desc/metadata defaults, builds ChangeSubscriptionWorkflowInput, calls planSubscriptionService.Change. Response is BillingSubscriptionChangeResponse{Current, Next} not a single subscription.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Metadata defaults come from the existing subscription; only override if body.Labels is non-nil.

key_file_guides.role: ToAPIBillingSubscription (uses clock.Now() for GetStatusAt), FromAPIBillingSubscriptionEditTiming (datetime-before-enum order), FromAPIBillingSubscriptionCreate.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Tests that assert subscription status must mock clock via clock.SetTime. The timing decode order (datetime first) is load-bearing — reversing it breaks custom RFC3339 timing.

key_file_guides.role: CancelSubscription; defaults timing to TimingImmediate when body.Timing is nil.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Nil timing body must default to immediate — do not require timing to be present.

anti_patterns: Swapping planSubscriptionService and subscriptionService calls — Create/Change must go through the plan service for billing sync hooks.
Reversing timing decode order (trying enum before datetime) — datetime strings will be misidentified as enum values.
Skipping getCustomerByIDOrKey / getPlanByIDOrKey pre-validation and calling planSubscriptionService directly — plan must be resolved to a concrete version before creating.
Using commonhttp.GenericErrorEncoder() instead of apierrors.GenericErrorEncoder() in v3 handlers.
Setting PlanInput fields directly instead of using planInput.FromRef — direct assignment bypasses the FromRef invariants.

decisions.decision: Two separate subscription services: planSubscriptionService and subscriptionService
decisions.rationale: plansubscription.PlanSubscriptionService orchestrates billing sync and plan versioning on top of the core subscription.Service; the split keeps billing-coupling out of the generic subscription lifecycle and allows non-plan subscriptions in the future.

decisions.decision: datetime decoded before enum in FromAPIBillingSubscriptionEditTiming
decisions.rationale: Both datetime strings and enum strings satisfy the string union type; trying datetime first and falling back to enum is the only way to correctly route RFC3339 custom timestamps without misidentifying them as enum values.

decisions.decision: Entity pre-validation (customer + plan lookup) happens in the decoder, not in the operation closure
decisions.rationale: Failing fast in the decoder returns structured 400 errors via apierrors.NewBadRequestError with typed InvalidParameters, giving callers actionable field-level error messages before any service call is made.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new subscription lifecycle action (e.g. PauseSubscription) following the established handler pattern
